meta_title: For Students: Antigravity project template to Solve no design skills (Intermediate Case Study)
meta_description: Learn how to Antigravity project template and overcome no design skills. A Intermediate-level Case Study using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: Antigravity Template to Solve No Design Skills
content.seo_description: Overcome a lack of design skills with the Antigravity project template. A practical, intermediate-level case study using Claude Code to build professional SaaS.
content.answer_block: The Antigravity project template provides a pre-architected, production-grade foundation that abstracts complex UI/UX requirements. By combining this modular framework with Claude Code’s autonomous generation, students can bypass traditional design hurdles, focusing instead on core logic while delivering high-fidelity, responsive applications that look like they were built by senior teams.
content.intro:
You’re staring at a blank screen, code logic flowing in your head, but the moment you touch CSS, the dream dies. Most students abandon brilliant projects simply because they lack the UI/UX polish required to make a SaaS feel legitimate. The frustration isn't in the coding; it's in the visual gap between your ambition and your output.

But what if you didn't have to 'design'? The Antigravity project template acts as a force multiplier, providing a design-system-first architecture that handles responsiveness, spacing, and typography automatically. When paired with Claude Code, you transition from a struggling student to an efficient builder, shipping enterprise-grade interfaces in hours, not weeks.

steps.title: Bootstrap the Antigravity Architecture
steps.description: Initialize your project using the core template to establish the design system and directory structure.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Initialize a new project using the Antigravity base template with Tailwind CSS and ShadcnUI components pre-configured for a SaaS dashboard.

steps.title: Define Schema and Data Models
steps.description: Establish the backbone of your app so the AI understands the relationship between your UI and your data.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a Prisma schema for a task management SaaS including User, Project, and Task models with appropriate relationships.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'generate prisma schema for task management with user-project-task relationships'

steps.title: Generate Modular UI Components
steps.description: Leverage the template's existing design tokens to build interface elements without writing custom CSS.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a responsive data table component using the Antigravity design system tokens for table headers and rows.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'create a responsive ShadcnUI table component based on the Antigravity design system'

steps.title: Integrate API Logic
steps.description: Connect your frontend to backend endpoints using the template's built-in API handler patterns.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a Next.js server action to fetch and filter tasks based on the Prisma schema defined in step 2.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'implement Next.js server actions for task CRUD operations'

steps.title: Polishing the User Flow
steps.description: Use Claude Code to review your layout against accessibility standards and ensure the design feels cohesive.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Review the current dashboard layout for accessibility compliance and ensure all spacing adheres to the Antigravity design system.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'audit UI components for accessibility and spacing consistency'

pro_tips: Never override Antigravity's base CSS variables; always extend them in the config to maintain global design consistency.
Use Claude Code to generate unit tests for your UI components immediately after creation to prevent regression during design tweaks.
Treat the Antigravity template as a 'source of truth'—if a feature doesn't fit the template's pattern, rethink your architecture.
Leverage the built-in 'mock data' generator in the template during development to test UI responsiveness without needing a live database.
Focus on 'State-Driven Design' where your UI reacts to data states; this eliminates the need for manual CSS styling.
content.next_steps: Once your MVP is deployed, begin adding custom features one by one, using Claude Code to refactor your code into reusable modules to keep your project scalable.
content.conversion_block: You now have the framework, the tools, and the workflow to move past the 'no design skills' barrier forever. Your portfolio deserves to look professional, and your code deserves to be modular. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
